brand.legal: Salt Lake City, Utah, USA · a Utah company
brand.titleLong: MurrayGreyTank — The Global Marketplace & Knowledge Hub for Murray Grey Genetics
brand.footerBlurb: the world's marketplace for Murray Grey cattle genetics
brand.description: Buy and sell Murray Grey cattle genetics — semen and embryos — and explore the breed's Australian origin story, foundation bloodlines, a live genetics price index, and translated global news. The central hub for the world's Murray Grey community. Free to list.

products.label: Semen
products.blurb: Conventional & sexed straws from proven Murray Grey sires.

products.label: Embryos
products.blurb: Full-blood IVF & in-vivo embryos, sire × dam pedigrees.

registry_names: Murray Grey Beef Cattle Society of Australia
vocab.help_nuance: Murray Greys are naturally polled cattle whose coat ranges from silver to dun to chocolate; they were developed in Australia's upper Murray River valley. Don't confuse them with other grey breeds (e.g. Grey Brahman or Charolais).

copy.heroTitle: The world's marketplace & knowledge hub for Murray Grey genetics
copy.heroSub: Buy and sell Murray Grey semen and embryos — and explore the breed's Australian origin story, foundation bloodlines, a live genetics price index, and translated global news. List in under a minute. Free.
copy.searchPlaceholder: Search a stud, sire, or bloodline — e.g. Willalooka, Monterey…
copy.roundupTitle: Every Murray Grey genetics listing, one feed
copy.roundupIntro: The Roundup brings the whole Murray Grey genetics market together: listings posted on MurrayGreyTank plus semen and embryo listings we track from studs' public pages across the web (marked 📡 — those link straight back to the original seller's page; web sellers pay nothing and can remove a listing anytime).
copy.theaterIntro: The world's Murray Grey video library — bulls on film, sale recordings, and how-to. Searchable by registration number.
copy.foundationIntro: Nearly all Murray Greys trace back to the silver calves born at Thologolong on the upper Murray River — and to Helen Sutherland, who made a breed from them. Meet the animals behind the breed.
